chunks.text: Paul N. Hilfinger is a Professor of Teaching Emeritus in EECS at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes 787 Soda Hall, phone 510-642-8401, and email hilfingr@cs.berkeley.edu. Links provided are Personal Homepage, Publications, Dissertations, and Research Support. Leslie Goldstein is listed with contact at 765 Soda, phone 3-2469, and email lgolds@berkeley.edu.

Paul N. Hilfinger earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Carnegie-Mellon University in 1980 and an AB in Mathematics from Princeton University in 1973. His research areas include Programming Systems (PS) and Scientific Computing (SCI), with a focus on software engineering and parallel programming techniques.

Selected publications by Paul N. Hilfinger include 'Titanium Language Reference Manual (Version 2.20)' from 2006 and 'Titanium: A high-performance Java dialect' published in Concurrency: Practice and Experience in 1998. These works involve collaborations with other researchers in the field.

Paul N. Hilfinger has received honors such as the Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science in 2000 and the Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science in 1993.
